marketState: bearish — broad multi-timeframe downtrend and storage surplus overhang, with a near-term oversold bounce risk
summary: Every technical timeframe from 5-minute to weekly shows a bearish trend with 50<200 EMA, and storage sits +184 Bcf above the 5-year average after a +72 Bcf bearish surprise build, confirming an established downtrend and oversupply backdrop. Price is running -15.4% YTD versus a -3.4% seasonal median (-12pp), and seasonality's own forward odds skew slightly down (31% chance higher in 4 weeks, typical move -3.1%) though reliability is flagged Low (50pp spread). The fair-value model still calls the front contract FAIR (spread only -$0.034 inside a $2.417-$3.417 band), so there is no large mispricing to lean on either side right now. Near-term weather is a genuine bright spot — demand is +16.4% vs 5y (rangePos 0.81) over the next week — but that support fades to +5.7% by days 8-16, and daily/4h RSI readings (33.4 / 22.9) plus a daily close below the lower Bollinger band point to oversold conditions that could spark a short-covering bounce even within the larger downtrend. Managed money is net short (-60,295 contracts) but only at the 35th percentile of the past 5 years, so the short trade is not crowded.

longTerm.entry: $2.90-2.95 (sell into a bounce toward the fair-value zone)
longTerm.rationale: Storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build, price sits well below its seasonal median (-15.4% YTD vs -3.4%), and seasonality's forward odds skew slightly bearish (31% higher in 4 weeks) even though reliability is Low. All six technical timeframes confirm a bearish trend structure (50<200 EMA), and managed-money shorts are not yet crowded (35th percentile), leaving room to extend the position.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained break back above the fair-value band midpoint (roughly $2.95-3.00) on rising volume, or a storage deficit surprise / sharp upside revision to the 8-16D weather demand outlook, would invalidate the short thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term signals conflict: hot 1-7D weather (+16.4% vs 5y, rangePos 0.81) argues for support, while price sits right on fair value (spread only -$0.034, no mispricing edge) and the intraday-to-daily trend structure is still bearish (1h trend bearish, 50<200 EMA) even as 4h/1h MACD and oversold RSI (22.9 / 33.4) hint at a bounce. That mix is not a clean edge in either direction for a 1-10 day trade.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a bounce into the $2.95-3.05 fair-value zone to sell (aligning with the long-term short), or a confirmed reversal — daily close back above the lower Bollinger band with RSI resetting off oversold and MACD turning positive on the daily — before taking a tactical long.

Managed-money net short (-60,295 contracts) but only 35th percentile — not crowded
risks: Weather forecasts can shift quickly; an extended heat wave would reverse the near-term demand tailwind and squeeze shorts
